prompt:
A hyper-realistic, high-angle photograph of a vast, chaotic industrial conveyer belt. The scene is dominated by perspective, stretching into a hazy background.

The conveyer belt is filled with tiny, indistinguishable, rapidly moving metallic widgets packed tightly in dozens of clustered segments. There are almost no gaps between them; they blur together in their numbers.

Interrupting this flow, there is a rare, colossal, massive, slow-moving void on the conveyer line—a gigantic, empty, rectangular segment that takes up 60% of the visible foreground frame. It is dark, vacant, and silent.

At the very front of the scene, a lone human observer is seen from behind. The observer is reaching out and their entire palm is flat against the rough surface of the gigantic, slow-moving empty void. Thousands of tiny widgets are visible flowing past just inches beyond the void, but the observer is stuck in the immense representation of the rare absence.

The lighting is harsh industrial spotlighting, casting long, stark shadows that make the structure feel oppressive. The textures are rough metal and concrete.

NO text, labels, numbers, formulas, glowing lines, charts, cyborgs, futuristic interfaces, or diagrams are visible in the image. The scene must look like a physical, tangible place.
visual_logic: Viewer sees an observer stuck touching a rare, monstrous empty void that dominates the foreground, even as thousands of tiny, frequent objects flow past just beyond it, visually embodying why the longest intervals are the ones sampled.
